cover.status_line: Confidential · For Internal Algolia Use Only

company_snapshot.revenue_source_label: Tapestry IR — FY2025 Full Year Results

executives.name: Joanne Crevoiserat
executives.title: Chief Executive Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed, data-driven organization, driving meaningful durable growth.
executives.quote_source_label: Tapestry Investor Day — Amplify Strategy Announcement
executives.relevance: CEO brands Tapestry as 'data-driven' yet site search has zero NLP capability — a direct gap between stated strategy and current technology.

executives.name: Joanne Crevoiserat
executives.title: Chief Executive Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: We are already applying AI tools across the value chain from product development, as you know, to inventory management, to pricing, to marketing, AI tools are embedded. What we've been focused on at Tapestry is putting the tools in the hands of the decision-makers in our organization, which is so critically important.
executives.quote_source_label: Motley Fool — Tapestry Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call Transcript, February 5, 2026
executives.relevance: AI across the value chain — but not site search. Search is still keyword-based while AI investment flows to product and marketing.
executives._internal_note: Quote from earnings call discussion, not press release. Verify transcript before citing verbatim.

executives.name: Yang Lu
executives.title: Chief Information & Digital Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Digital will continue to be really important for us, because at the end of the day, we want to meet our customers where they are, and our customer is an omnichannel customer. [Success requires] persistent, relentless experimentation and building a culture of test and learn. Iteration is the new perfection.
executives.relevance: 'Meet customers where they are' — yet search cannot resolve multi-intent queries like 'gift for mom under 300'. The omnichannel vision requires AI-powered search, not SFCC native.

executives.name: Fabio Luzzi
executives.title: Chief Data & Analytics Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: This marks an important milestone in Tapestry's journey to make data and AI a true competitive advantage. Over the past few years, we've built a world class data foundation — culminating in our patented Data Fabric, which powers everything from advanced analytics to AI and GenAI across our brands.
executives.quote_source_label: Retail Technology Innovation Hub — CDAO Appointment, Nov 2025
executives.relevance: Data Fabric powers GenAI across brands — but site search is a blind spot. Algolia search analytics would feed signal back into the Data Fabric for the first time.

executives.name: Noam Paransky
executives.title: former Chief Omni and Innovation Officer, Tapestry Inc. (departed Sept 2024; now Strategic Advisor, Intelo.ai)
executives.quote: Amperity allows us to better understand our customers and put actionable insights into the hands of our teams to drive experimentation and activation, enabling us to personalize the critical touchpoints our brands have with our customers without the need for significant technology expertise or code.
executives.quote_source_label: Consumer Goods Technology — Tapestry Personalized Experiences, 2023
executives.relevance: 'Personalize critical touchpoints without code' — site search is not yet one of those touchpoints. Amperity has the customer data; Algolia delivers the personalized search experience on top.

executives.name: Scott Roe
executives.title: Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Sending 13 emails after someone visits your store doesn't build loyalty. It's about making the first interaction meaningful, then communicating only when we have something relevant to say.
executives.relevance: CFO articulates 'first interaction meaningful' — when a new customer's first search returns 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300', that first interaction is lost.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Joanne Crevoiserat: 'Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed, data-driven organization.' Yet site search returned 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300' — a direct gap between stated strategy and live technology.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO — Tapestry Investor Day
intelligence_signals.relevance: CEO's 'data-driven' brand promise is undermined by a 1/10 NLP search score. Algolia closes this gap — making the CEO's strategy credible.

intelligence_signals.text: CIO Yang Lu grew Tapestry digital revenue significantly over 9 years as CIO (precise figures: verify with Retail Tech Insights before citing). Post-merger-collapse, focus is now 'innovative technologies including AI'. H1 FY2026 CapEx + cloud computing spend was $92M (vs $69M prior year) — run-rate implies ~$180-200M annual IT/digital investment.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Tapestry Q2 FY2026 Earnings Release — confirmed $92M H1 CapEx+Cloud
intelligence_signals.relevance: Budget exists ($80M of $200M CapEx to IT/digital). The Capri merger collapse freed Yang Lu's agenda to focus on organic AI innovation — prime window for an Algolia conversation.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry partnered with Lily AI to enrich product attributes with consumer-language tagging — helping products surface more accurately in search and SEO. Despite this investment, the live audit shows zero NLP capability for multi-intent queries like "gift for mom under 300" (1 result). Lily AI solves the catalog side; Algolia solves the query side.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Tapestry has already invested in AI-powered product discovery. The gap that remains is query understanding — what customers type vs. what the engine interprets. Lily AI cannot address this. Algolia NeuralSearch can.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry Q2 FY2026 EPS beat by 21% ($2.69 vs $2.22 forecast). Digital revenue +20% YoY. FY2026 revenue guidance raised to ~$7.3B. No hiring freeze. 751 open roles.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Strong financial health with growing digital revenue = budget and appetite for infrastructure that amplifies the momentum. Algolia ROI case is easy: 5% lift on $367.5M = $18.4M/year.

intelligence_signals.text: 60% of new Coach customer acquisitions are Gen Z and Millennials. Coach acquired 6.8 million new customers in FY2025. Gen Z expects conversational, semantic search — the exact capability that failed in the audit.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Tapestry FY2025 Full Year Results — Q4 Earnings Release
intelligence_signals.relevance: Coach's fastest-growing segment (Gen Z) is the most likely to abandon a site after a failed NLP search. Every Gen Z 'gift for mom' query that fails is a lost acquisition.

intelligence_signals.text: Michael Kors — Coach's closest accessible luxury competitor, same SFCC platform — already runs Algolia (confirmed via BuiltWith, March 2026). Tory Burch, Marc Jacobs, Furla, and Longchamp do not yet use Algolia. Coach can join Michael Kors ahead of the other four.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Michael Kors is the validation story: same platform (SFCC), same product category (handbags), already chose Algolia. This removes the "unproven in our segment" objection entirely. Ask Algolia for the Michael Kors reference.

intelligence_signals.text: Coach plans 60+ new stores in China over 3 years, expanding from 400+ stores to 100+ cities. China business grew 19% YoY in Q1 FY2026. Coach ranked #1 brand during Double 11.
intelligence_signals.relevance: China expansion requires multilingual search (Chinese characters verified as top organic keyword), geo-specific merchandising, and consistent sub-50ms latency across regions — all SFCC native gaps.

intelligence_signals.text: VP eCommerce (Kate Spade, $424M revenue scope) and Director Personalization ($130–150K) were both recently filled. Active open roles include Manager, Product Management (Omnichannel) and Senior Digital Product Manager (MarTech, Shanghai). Zero search engineer, relevance engineer, or Elasticsearch roles found.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Incoming VP eCommerce = new decision-maker who will review the search stack. No in-house search engineering = buy-not-build posture confirmed. Optimal outreach window: now, before new hires establish vendor relationships.

intelligence_signals.text: New CDAO Fabio Luzzi: 'We've built a world class data foundation — culminating in our patented Data Fabric, which powers everything from advanced analytics to AI and GenAI across our brands.'
intelligence_signals.source_name: Retail Technology Innovation Hub — CDAO Appointment
intelligence_signals.relevance: Data Fabric is the strategic backbone — but search analytics is a blind spot. Algolia Analytics would feed real-time first-party search signal (zero-result queries, click-through, session data) into the Data Fabric.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry recently filled a Director, Search (SEO and AEO) role. The inclusion of AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) in the job title signals active investment in AI-powered search visibility — exactly the capability Algolia NeuralSearch provides.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Tapestry Careers — Director, Search (SEO and AEO)
intelligence_signals.relevance: AEO = Answer Engine Optimization (LLM/AI search). Tapestry is already investing in AI search visibility externally — Algolia addresses the on-site AI search gap that complements this strategy.

competitors.name: Michael Kors
competitors.notes: CONFIRMED: Algolia detected via BuiltWith (March 2026). Previously on Oracle Commerce/ATG (Pivotree case study), now migrated to SFCC + Algolia. Same platform stack as Coach. Source: BuiltWith domain-lookup 2026-03-18.

competitors.name: Tory Burch
competitors.notes: SFCC Einstein native search. Same platform as Coach. Limited customization, locked to Salesforce ecosystem.

competitors.name: Marc Jacobs
competitors.notes: SFCC Einstein. Part of LVMH portfolio. LVMH reportedly exploring sale of Marc Jacobs for ~$1B.

competitors.name: Furla
competitors.notes: Recent SFCC migration (go-live Sept 2022). Still maturing on platform.

competitors.name: Longchamp
competitors.notes: Shopify (confirmed via BuiltWith). VisibleOne case study shows custom PHP/Laravel front-end, but e-commerce search layer is Shopify native — weakest of all five competitors. No AI capability.

findings.title: Zero Semantic/NLP Search — Conversational Queries Fail
findings.expected_behavior: 100+ gift-appropriate products under $300 for women — handbags, wallets, accessories filtered by price
findings.actual_behavior: 1 result: 'Sammy Watch Gift Set, 22.5 Mm X 28 Mm — $195'. Keyword-matched on 'gift' only. No understanding of recipient, occasion, or price constraint in complex NLP context.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of e-commerce sites fail to fully support 8 key search query types used by customers
findings.prospect_description: When a customer searches 'gift for mom under 300', your search found only 1 product — a watch with 'Gift Set' in the name. Gen Z customers expect to search the way they talk. This one fails completely.
findings.algolia_solution: NeuralSearch with hybrid keyword + vector search. Algolia's Query Understanding parses intent (gift-giving), recipient (mom/women), and price constraint ($300) simultaneously — returning hundreds of relevant products.

findings.title: No On-Site Personalization — Every Visitor Treated Identically
findings.expected_behavior: Search results personalized by browsing history, purchase history, and customer segment (VIP vs. first-time vs. Gen Z)
findings.actual_behavior: Static facets (Categories, Bag Categories, Gender, Price, Color, Bag Size, Percent Off, Size, Material) with no visible personalization signals. Bluecore handles email only — on-site search personalization is absent.
findings.prospect_description: A VIP customer who has bought Coach bags for 10 years sees the exact same search results as a first-time visitor. Your Amperity CDP has rich customer data that's not flowing into search.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Personalization API + Recommend. User profiles built from click, purchase, and session data deliver ranked results specific to each shopper. Integrates directly with your existing Amperity CDP.

findings.title: Poor No-Results Handling — Random Products Instead of Guidance
findings.expected_behavior: Clear 'no results found' message with helpful alternatives: popular products, related categories, or 'Did you mean?' suggestions
findings.actual_behavior: 4 random irrelevant products displayed (Signature Rainbow Scarf, Restored Sleeveless Dress, Bone Single Earring, Sterling Silver Pendant). No 'Did you mean?', no popular products, no search tips. Confusing rather than helpful.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer misspells or searches for something you don't carry, they get four random products with no explanation. This is a dead end that sends shoppers to competitors.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions + Rules Engine for empty-state recovery. Surfaces popular products, suggests related categories, and guides users back into the shopping funnel instead of showing random results.

findings.title: Intent Detection Incomplete — Complex Multi-Attribute Queries Fail
findings.expected_behavior: Parse multiple intent signals simultaneously: gifting occasion + female recipient + price ceiling
findings.actual_behavior: Simple price intent works ('purses under 300' → 218 results with max $295 filter). Complex NLP combining price + semantic intent fails completely. Only single-attribute intent understood.
findings.prospect_description: Your search handles simple requests but breaks when customers combine occasion, recipient, and budget in one query — exactly how Gen Z searches.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Understanding with multi-intent parsing. Handles occasion + recipient + price simultaneously, routing to contextually relevant product sets with real-time facet application.

findings.title: Typo Tolerance: Strong — 565 Results for Misspelled Query
findings.expected_behavior: Correct misspelling and return relevant handbag results
findings.actual_behavior: 565 relevant handbag results for 'handbagg'. Products: Brooklyn Shoulder Bag ($395), Chelsea Shoulder Bag ($325), Juliet Shoulder Bag ($375), Plaza Bag ($125). Better than expected for SFCC native — likely enhanced by Lily AI attribute enrichment.
findings.prospect_description: Typo tolerance is working well — customers who misspell product names still find what they're looking for.
findings.algolia_solution: This is a strength. Algolia would maintain typo tolerance while adding the NLP and personalization layers that are currently missing.

findings.title: Query Suggestions: Popular Searches Active in Empty State
findings.tested_query: (empty — search bar opened with no text)
findings.actual_behavior: 'BEST-SELLING PRODUCTS' section + 'Popular Searches': Wallet, Tote, Shoulder Bag, Tabby, Brooklyn. Good UX scaffolding for new visitors entering the search experience.
findings.prospect_description: Empty state is well-handled — new visitors get helpful guidance before they type anything.
findings.algolia_solution: This is a strength. Algolia Query Suggestions would layer trending and personalized suggestions on top of the existing popular terms.

findings.title: Federated Content Search: Non-Product Queries Return Help Pages
findings.expected_behavior: Return help/policy content, not just products
findings.actual_behavior: Full redirect to Returns & Exchanges help page showing Order Status, Returns & Exchanges, Coach Cares, FAQ sections, and return policy table with timeframes. Content is indexed and searchable.
findings.prospect_description: Post-purchase service queries route correctly to help content — a good foundation for federated search.
findings.algolia_solution: This is a strength. Algolia Federated Search would unify products, help content, store locator, and loyalty program data in a single search experience.

findings.title: SAYT Latency: Fast Autocomplete — Product Thumbnails in <200ms
findings.actual_behavior: 'TOP PRODUCTS' dropdown with product thumbnails and 'Top Searches' suggestions (Handbag, Handbags) appearing within ~200ms of typing. Fast, visual, and merchandised.
findings.prospect_description: SAYT performance is solid — products appear quickly as customers type.
findings.algolia_solution: This is a strength. Algolia sub-50ms indexing would maintain this performance while adding NLP relevance that current SFCC native lacks.

gap_pairs.said_quote: Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed, data-driven organization, driving meaningful durable growth.
gap_pairs.found_title: NLP Query Returns 1 Result — Not Data-Driven
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Query 'gift for mom under 300' → 1 result (screenshot: screenshots/06-nlp-gift-query.png). No search analytics, no personalization, no intent detection.

gap_pairs.said_quote: Amperity allows us to better understand our customers and put actionable insights into the hands of our teams to drive experimentation and activation, enabling us to personalize the critical touchpoints our brands have with our customers.
gap_pairs.said_attr: Noam Paransky, former Chief Digital Officer, Tapestry Inc.
gap_pairs.found_title: Site Search Has Zero Personalization Signals
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Query 'bags' → static identical results regardless of customer history. No personalization visible in SAYT, results page, or facets (screenshot: screenshots/15-facets-bags.png).

gap_pairs.said_quote: Digital will continue to be really important for us, because at the end of the day, we want to meet our customers where they are, and our customer is an omnichannel customer.
gap_pairs.said_attr: Yang Lu, Chief Information & Digital Officer
gap_pairs.found_title: Conversational Search Fails — Customers Not Met 'Where They Are'
gap_pairs.found_evidence: Gen Z customers use natural language. 'Gift for mom under 300' → 1 result. The omnichannel promise breaks at the most common Gen Z entry point (screenshot: screenshots/06-nlp-gift-query.png).
gap_pairs._internal_note: Exact quote text not confirmed at NRF blog URL; sourced from Tapestry investor intelligence scratchpad (11-investor-intelligence.md). Verify transcript before citing.

gap_pairs.said_quote: The most likely chance of getting a second date is how good the first date was.
gap_pairs.found_title: First Search Experience Fails 60% of New Customers (Gen Z)
gap_pairs.found_evidence: 60% of new Coach acquisitions are Gen Z/Millennials who search conversationally. First NLP search on coach.com returns 1 result — the worst possible 'first date' impression (screenshot: screenshots/06-nlp-gift-query.png).

revenue_3y.signal: Merger attempt with Capri blocked; digital $2B milestone
Record revenue; Stuart Weitzman divested; Amplify strategy launched

hiring.build_vs_buy: BUY — Zero search/relevance/Elasticsearch roles found. No in-house search team. Tapestry relies on vendor partnerships (Lily AI, Certona, Bluecore) for all digital experience capabilities.

objection_counters.objection: We already use Lily AI for search
objection_counters.counter: Lily AI enriches your product catalog — attributes, consumer-language tags. Algolia solves the query side: understanding what customers mean when they type 'gift for mom under 300'. They're complementary, not competitive. Lily AI feeds better data in; Algolia delivers better results out.

objection_counters.objection: We're on SFCC and don't want to change platforms
objection_counters.counter: Algolia doesn't replace SFCC — it layers on top with a pre-built cartridge. Your catalog stays in SFCC. Algolia handles the search index, ranking, and UX. It's a 2–3 week integration, not a platform migration.

SFCC Einstein is built for recommendations, not search. Your audit shows this — 'gift for mom under 300' returns 1 result on Einstein-powered SFCC. Algolia NeuralSearch would return hundreds. It's a different product category.

strategic_angles.label: The NLP Gap — Gen Z's Language, Not Understood
strategic_angles.hook: 60% of new Coach customers are Gen Z. They search the way they talk. Your search doesn't understand them.
pain_points: 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300' — a core Gen Z use case
Zero semantic/natural language processing despite Lily AI partnership
6.8M new customers acquired in FY2025 — conversion lost at first NLP search
strategic_angles.discovery_question: We found that 'gift for mom under 300' returns 1 result on coach.com. How many gift-intent searches fail like this during Holiday and Mother's Day peaks — and what does that cost in abandoned sessions?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Lacoste: +37% Conversion Rate after NeuralSearch deployment (https://www.algolia.com/customers/lacoste)

strategic_angles.label: The Personalization Gap — $2B in Customer Data, Not Used at Search
strategic_angles.hook: Tapestry has invested in Amperity, Bluecore, AWS Bedrock, and a patented Data Fabric. None of it flows into on-site search personalization.
pain_points: Amperity CDP has rich customer profiles — not connected to search
Bluecore personalizes email but not the search results page
VIP customers and first-time visitors see identical search results
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Is your Amperity customer data currently informing what search results a returning VIP customer sees, versus a first-time visitor? Or is search still treating everyone the same?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Harry Rosen: 360% increase in conversion rate with Algolia Personalization (https://www.algolia.com/customers/harry-rosen)

strategic_angles.label: The Validation Angle — Michael Kors Already Chose Algolia on SFCC
strategic_angles.hook: Michael Kors — your closest competitor, same SFCC platform — already runs Algolia. You have proof of concept within your own competitive set.
pain_points: Michael Kors confirmed using Algolia on SFCC (BuiltWith, March 2026) — same stack as Coach
Tory Burch, Marc Jacobs, Furla still on SFCC Einstein native — same gap Coach has today
Coach can leapfrog three SFCC-native competitors while citing a same-segment peer as proof
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Michael Kors — your closest accessible luxury competitor on the same SFCC platform — already runs Algolia. Are you aware of that, and does it change how you think about the timeline for this decision?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Michael Kors: SFCC + Algolia confirmed (BuiltWith March 2026). Lacoste: +37% Conversion Rate (https://www.algolia.com/customers/lacoste)

strategic_angles.label: The Data Fabric Angle — Search as a Signal Source
strategic_angles.hook: Fabio Luzzi's Data Fabric powers AI across Tapestry. Site search is a blind spot — no signal flows from search into the fabric.
pain_points: Zero-result queries are not captured or acted upon
Click-through rates, abandonment, and session data are invisible to the Data Fabric
Lily AI optimizes catalog attributes but search analytics don't feed back to merchandising
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your Data Fabric powers analytics and AI across the business. Does site search feed signal back into that fabric today — or is search a data black hole?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia Analytics Dashboard captures zero-result queries, click-through rates, and search session data in real time — directly integrable with AWS data pipelines.

priority_to_product.discovery_question: Joanne mentioned being 'consumer-obsessed and data-driven' at Investor Day. How are you currently using search analytics to understand what customers search for but don't find?

priority_to_product.discovery_question: Noam talked about insights 'in the hands of teams without code.' How empowered are your merchandising teams today to adjust search results without engineering tickets?

priority_to_product.their_priority: AI tools across the value chain — putting tools in hands of decision-makers
priority_to_product.source: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO — Q2 FY2026 Earnings, Feb 2026
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Joanne mentioned AI across product, inventory, pricing, and marketing. Is search and discovery part of that AI roadmap, or is it still keyword-based?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Persistent, relentless experimentation — iteration is the new perfection
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Yang's philosophy is 'iteration is the new perfection.' How frequently are you A/B testing search algorithms today — weekly, monthly, or is that a manual process?

anchor_lines: Tapestry told investors it's 'consumer-obsessed and data-driven' — we can make that true at the search bar with NeuralSearch.
Tapestry invested in Lily AI to improve product discoverability. 'Gift for mom under 300' still returns 1 result. Algolia solves the query-understanding side that Lily AI cannot.
Yang Lu grew Tapestry's digital revenue significantly over 9 years as CIO.  The next $500M comes from search that understands how Gen Z shops.
Fabio Luzzi's Data Fabric is world-class — but site search is its only blind spot. Algolia Analytics closes the loop.

outreach_sequence.message_hook: Reference his NRF 2026 session on unified commerce. Attach audit screenshot of 'gift for mom under 300' returning 1 result. Ask if this is a known gap or a surprise.

Reference Fortune article on his post-merger AI focus. Frame Algolia as the AI layer for the one part of the value chain not yet covered: on-site search. Lead with ROI: $18.4–36.8M/year at 5–10% lift.
Welcome to the role. They've been hired to close the personalization gap — Algolia is the on-site search layer that completes the Amperity + Bluecore + Algolia personalization stack.

competitive_synthesis.scenario: Tapestry's five key competitors all use legacy native platform search (SFCC Einstein, Oracle Endeca, Shopify native). Zero use Algolia. First-mover opportunity in accessible luxury is clear and uncontested.

competitor_tiers.note: Confirmed Algolia on SFCC via BuiltWith (March 2026). Direct proof of concept: same platform stack as Coach, same accessible luxury segment. Ask your Algolia AE for the Michael Kors reference story.

competitor_tiers.tier: SFCC Native — Same Gap as Coach
competitor_tiers.note: All on SFCC without Algolia — identical search limitations to Coach pre-upgrade. Coach + Algolia would outperform all three simultaneously.
Shopify native search (BuiltWith confirmed). Weakest search of the group.

competitive_synthesis.golden_angle: 1 of 5 Coach competitors (Michael Kors) already uses Algolia on Salesforce Commerce Cloud — the exact same platform stack as Coach. This is proof of concept within the accessible luxury segment: SFCC + Algolia works for handbags and accessories at scale. Tory Burch, Marc Jacobs, Furla, and Longchamp do not yet use Algolia — Coach can join Michael Kors ahead of the other four.

positioning_statements.vs_sfcc_einstein: Michael Kors — your closest accessible luxury competitor — already runs SFCC + Algolia. Tory Burch, Marc Jacobs, and Furla are still on SFCC Einstein native. NeuralSearch is the upgrade that separates market leaders from followers. "Gift for mom under 300" returns 1 result on Einstein and 100+ on Algolia.
positioning_statements.vs_lily_ai: Lily AI solves the catalog side (enriching product attributes so they're more discoverable). Algolia solves the query side (understanding what customers mean when they type). They are complementary — Lily AI feeds better data in, Algolia delivers better results out.
positioning_statements.vs_build: Tapestry has zero search engineers on staff and zero Elasticsearch/relevance roles posted. The buy-not-build posture is already confirmed. Algolia's SFCC cartridge is pre-built — no platform migration, catalog stays in SFCC.

ae_fields.urgency_reason: VP eCommerce (Kate Spade) and Director Personalization roles recently filled — new hires are in their first 90 days, open to vendor recommendations before preferences set. Also: Director, Search (SEO and AEO) recently hired, signaling active AI search investment. Monitor LinkedIn for new hire identities.
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: I reviewed coach.com with an AI-powered search audit last week. When I searched 'gift for mom under 300' — a query your Gen Z customers run every day — I got 1 result: a watch. I wanted to share what I found and get your read on whether that's a known gap or a surprise.
ae_fields.cta: 30-minute discovery call to walk through 3 audit findings + show what NeuralSearch would return for the same query.

next_steps.title: Request 30-Minute Discovery Call with Mandeep Bhatia
next_steps.description: Warmest entry point via NRF 2026 shared context. Lead with the NLP audit finding — screenshot of 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300'. Ask if this represents the known search gap Lily AI was meant to solve.

next_steps.title: Monitor for New Director Personalization Hire (Role Recently Filled)
next_steps.description: The Director, Personalization & Communication role was confirmed FILLED as of March 2026. Monitor careers.tapestry.com and LinkedIn for the new hire's identity. First 30 days remains the optimal outreach window — new hire needs to build the on-site personalization stack and Algolia is the missing layer.

next_steps.title: Prepare Pilot Proposal: NeuralSearch + Personalization on coach.com
next_steps.description: Build a 90-day POC scoped to Coach US search (not Kate Spade yet). KPIs: NLP query success rate, search conversion rate, zero-results rate. Use $36.8M/year moderate ROI scenario as the business case anchor. Align timing with Yang Lu's FY2026 $80M IT/digital CapEx budget cycle.
